description: Potential data race: shared mutable state accessed from multiple threads without synchronization
rationale: Data races are undefined behavior; all cross-thread mutable access must use Mutex, RwLock, or atomic operations
pattern: SharedMut(x) across Thread(t1), Thread(t2) without Lock
context: x: !Send + Sync, or shared without Arc<Mutex>
alternatives: Use atomic types for simple values

description: Potential deadlock: multiple locks acquired in inconsistent order
rationale: Acquiring locks A then B in one thread and B then A in another can cause both threads to wait forever
pattern: Lock(a); Lock(b) in Thread(t1); Lock(b); Lock(a) in Thread(t2)
fix_template: Always acquire locks in a consistent global order (e.g. by address or ID)

description: Type does not implement Send but is transferred across thread boundary
rationale: Only Send types can be safely moved between threads; non-Send types may contain thread-local state
fix_template: Use a Send-compatible wrapper or clone the data before spawning
alternatives: Keep the data on the original thread and use channels

description: Type does not implement Sync but is shared across threads via reference
rationale: Only Sync types can be safely referenced from multiple threads simultaneously
pattern: Share(&x) across Thread(t1), Thread(t2) where typeof(x): !Sync
fix_template: Wrap in a Sync-compatible type (Mutex<T>, RwLock<T>, or atomic)
alternatives: Clone the data for each thread

description: Lock held for an extended scope; may cause contention under high concurrency
rationale: Holding locks for long periods blocks other threads and reduces parallelism
fix_template: Minimize the critical section: extract non-shared work outside the lock

description: Unbounded channel may grow without limit under load
rationale: Unbounded channels can cause memory exhaustion if producers outpace consumers
fix_template: Use a bounded channel with appropriate capacity: `Channel::bounded(1024)`

description: Spawned task/thread is never joined; results may be silently lost
rationale: Not joining a spawned task means errors, panics, or results are silently discarded

description: Blocking operation called inside async context
rationale: Blocking calls in async code starve the executor's thread pool, causing deadlocks or poor performance
fix_template: Use the async equivalent or wrap in `spawn_blocking()`
alternatives: Move to a dedicated blocking thread pool
